text:
Once upon a time, there was a clever fox who lived near a big oak tree. Every day, the fox gathered acorns to share with his friends.

One day, while chattering away to his squirrel buddies, the fox noticed something unusual. Across the plains, a group of people was looking up at the mighty oak tree.

"Look!" exclamed the fox. "That's someone climbing a ladder!"

Soon enough, two humans tugged at the heavy branches, climbing higher and higher. Soon they reached the tippy-top.

"Why's she pointing?" the fox asked.

"They think they can catch an acorn?"

"Well," chuckled the fox, "what do you say?"

With a wink, he grabbed an acorn, popped it into his mouth, and said, "To all of us!"

One by one, the humans caught acorns, and the fox shared his bounty with them. And the oak tree prospered!

From that day forward, every year the villagers would help
